source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure Amazon VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) has been created

input_row.source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure Amazon VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) has been created
input_row.description: Amazon VPCs allow you to launch AWS resources into a defined virtual network, providing network isolation and controlling inbound and outbound traffic. Here’s a step- by-step guide on how to create an Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (VPC):
input_row.rationale: Impact: User would be required to have an AWS account to access AWS resources.
input_row.audit: 1. Sign in to the AWS Management Console Sign in to the AWS Management Console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/ with your AWS account credentials. 2. Open the Amazon VPC Console Navigate to the service using the Find Services search bar or by directly accessing the console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/vpc/. 3. Create a VPC In the Amazon VPC console, click Your VPCs in the left-hand navigation menu. • Click on the Create VPC button. • Provide a name and CIDR block for your VPC. The CIDR block defines the IP address range for your VPC. • Configure any additional settings, such as enabling DNS hostnames or DNS resolution. • Click on the Create button to create the VPC. 4. Create Subnets In the Amazon VPC console • Click on Subnets in the navigation menu. • Click on the Create subnet button. • Select the VPC you created in the previous step. • Provide a name and CIDR block for the subnet. The CIDR block should be a subset of the VPC's CIDR block. • Specify the availability zone where you want the subnet to be located. • Click on the Create button to create the subnet.  • Repeat these steps to create additional subnets if needed. 5. Create Security Groups In the Amazon VPC console, click Security Groups in the navigation menu. • Click on the Create security group button. • Provide a name and description for the security group. • Select the VPC you created earlier. • Configure inbound and outbound rules to allow the necessary network traffic for your Aurora database. • Click on the Create button to create the security group.
input_row.remediation: Follow the AWS documentation and create an AWS account to create a VPC (Virtual Private Cloud). References: 1. https://aws.amazon.com/products/databases/ 2. https://console.aws.amazon.com/vpc/
input_row.impact: User would be required to have an AWS account to access AWS resources.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- This control relates to the creation of an Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (VPC), which is a standard AWS service property.
- AWS APIs, particularly the `boto3` SDK and AWS CLI, provide methods to query and verify the existence of VPCs.
- The compliance check involves determining if at least one VPC exists within an AWS account, which can be easily automated using AWS APIs.
- The rationale and impact statements do not indicate any organizational policy requirement that would mandate a manual review, and there is no need for human judgment beyond what can be surfaced through API queries.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_vpc_existence_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the `boto3` Python SDK, specifically the `describe_vpcs` method, to list all VPCs in an account.
- AWS CLI command: `aws ec2 describe-vpcs` can also be used to retrieve the VPC list.
- Check if the `Vpcs` list returned by the API is empty or not. 
- Pass Criteria: At least one VPC should be present in the account.
- If there are no VPCs, it indicates non-compliance. If one or more VPCs are present, the control passes.
- Enumerate VPCs across all AWS regions accessible to the account, because VPCs are region-specific and compliance must be checked for each region individually.

**SOURCE:** CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.0.0
